ground.what_this_is: This is a 'Walkthrough' view where the system has automatically focused the user's attention on a specific item on the canvas, dimming everything else to highlight it.
ground.what_you_can_do: You can approve, reject, decide, comment, or skip the current item; you can also speak to the system to push a voice command.

howto.how_to_change: The surface is RhmChat.tsx; the action logic is driven by the RUN-run feature.
howto.what: The run command on the chat interface.
howto.what_you_can_do: Initiate a run session for the current chat context.

howto.how_to_change: The surface is the chat region (RhmChat.tsx); the node definitions are in suite.py:chat.
howto.what: The node palette on the walkthrough screen.
howto.what_you_can_do: Select a node kind to add to the current walkthrough session.

howto.how_to_change: The surface is the Walkthrough region (canvas/app/src/regions/Inbox.tsx); the live state and interaction logic are driven by suite.py:present_current.
howto.what: The active process item currently highlighted in the Walkthrough view.
howto.what_you_can_do: Approve, reject, decide, comment, or skip this specific process step; you can also speak to the system to push a voice command regarding it.

howto.how_to_change: The text is generated by the memo gate logic in runtime/scheduler.py; changing the visible text requires modifying that logic or the input hash conditions.
howto.what: The system's auto-generated explanation for why a node was skipped during the review.
howto.what_you_can_do: Read the technical justification (hash unchanged) and the code reference (runtime/scheduler.py) to understand the skip reason; you can then approve the skip or request a re-run.

howto.how_to_change: The trigger is the span region (Walkthrough.tsx); the re-run logic is suite.py:force (driven by the prompt expansion).

howto.what_you_can_do: Initiate a re-run of the current walkthrough session with an expanded prompt that explicitly includes the 'VOLATILE bypass' instruction.
